description_long: This church is a large, rectangular structure with a prominent bell tower featuring a steep, pointed roof and a weather vane at the top. The building is constructed primarily from reddish-brown stone or brick, with a series of pointed arch windows and decorative buttresses along the sides, suggesting a Gothic Revival architectural influence. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in light gray material, possibly slate or tile, and is dusted with what appears to be snow. The main entrance is located at the base of the bell tower, while additional smaller entrances and annexes are visible on the sides and rear. The church sits on a landscaped base with green grassy areas and paved walkways surrounding it. Ornamental details, such as finials and cross motifs, are present on the roof peaks and tower.
description: A large, reddish-brown stone church with a tall bell tower, steep gray roof, pointed arch windows, and decorative buttresses, set on a landscaped base.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the church, the tall bell tower on the left, and the main entrance at the tower base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the apse, smaller annex, and the symmetrical arrangement of the roof and buttresses.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the church, the bell tower on the right, and additional entrances and windows.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the main entrance, the base of the bell tower, and the symmetrical facade.
